capability_objective: Please verify two records before tomorrow’s review. Search separately for “Clinic backup line” in Health Account and “Museum guest Wi-Fi plan” in Arts Account, issuing those independent searches together in one assistant action. Only after each search returns a unique ID, retrieve both complete subscription records together in a later action and compare their status and date. Do not update, cancel, or notify either record. Report only what the retrieved records establish.
prompt:
Verify two subscription records in the provided sandbox before tomorrow's review. Read README.md first, then use the genuine local `./subscription-registry` executable it documents through Pi's Bash tool. Treat that executable as the only supported interface to subscriptions. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing data, generated audit evidence, or anything under `.harness`; do not modify those files or manufacture operation evidence. Do not modify README.md or .gitignore.

Search for the exact subscription name `Clinic backup line` in `Health Account` and the exact subscription name `Museum guest Wi-Fi plan` in `Arts Account`. The searches are independent. In the first assistant action that invokes the registry, issue exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls together, one direct search operation per call. Do not combine both searches in one shell call, use a broad search, or launch background jobs yourself. Wait for both results. Continue only if each search returns exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID. If either search does not resolve uniquely, stop before every retrieval, identify the unresolved lookup, and do not create the report.

Only after both search results have returned and resolved uniquely, use the next assistant action that invokes the registry to issue exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls together, one direct get operation per call. Retrieve each complete subscription using only the ID returned by its corresponding search. Do not guess an ID, retrieve a distractor, combine the gets in one shell call, or begin either get before both searches have finished. Compare status and date only from the complete records returned by get, never from search summaries or any other source.

This verification is read-only. Invoke no registry operation except the two required searches and the two eligible gets. Do not update, cancel, or notify either subscription or any other record. Do not alter registry state, backing data, audit evidence, or protected verification files, and do not manually create, edit, replay, or delete audit events. Leave no scratch artifacts.

After both complete records are returned, write `subscription_check.md` as the only new file. It must contain exactly three newline-terminated plain-text sentences, one per line, with no heading, bullets, blank lines, IDs, notes, interpretations, or recommendations. Put Clinic backup line first and Museum guest Wi-Fi plan second. Format each record line exactly as `<name> in <account> has status <status> and date <date>.`, preserving all four fields exactly as returned by that complete record. Format the third line as `The returned statuses <match-or-differ>, and the returned dates <match-or-differ>.`, replacing each placeholder with `match` or `differ` as established by the complete records. Return exactly the file content as the final response.
